# The L0 Guy

> Technical content that turns protocols into integrations. A writer who understands protocol internals AND can write them clearly — for the developers and the coding agents now building from docs. Also: the agent cost of your docs, measured in dollars, not guessed.

Name: The L0 Guy (handle). Two years inside LayerZero (Legend role, community council member). Builder of OFT Sentinel, winner of Mantle's Turing Test Hackathon ($17K, 576 entries).
Site: https://damli40.github.io/thel0guy/

## Offer

Technical content for crypto-infrastructure teams: I make complex protocol mechanics impossible to misunderstand — for the humans reading the docs and for the coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor) that now fetch those docs to write integrations. Lead offer: the agent cost audit — I run your docs and toolchain through a controlled multi-arm agent harness and hand you a measured table of what your current doc surface costs an agent per working integration, in dollars and minutes, then build the curated layer that fixes it. The audit runs across the frontier models your developers actually use: you pick up to three labs (Claude, GPT, Gemini), one flagship coding model each, and get a per-model table showing which one your docs trip up. $1,500 flat, one week. Report lands in seven days or the audit is free. Fee credits 100% into a docs-layer build within 60 days. Money-back if the table shows nothing worth fixing. After you ship the fixes, I re-run the harness within 30 days at no charge to confirm the cost dropped. Details: https://damli40.github.io/thel0guy/audit.html. Single conversion action: get in touch.

## The build (what the audit credits into)

The docs-layer build turns the audit's findings into a curated, agent-readable layer, then reruns the harness to prove the cost dropped. Three tiers: Targeted ($8K, ~2 weeks, the single highest-cost path from the audit, curated and re-measured), Complete ($14K, ~4 weeks, the full agent-readable layer across the doc surface plus an integration guide rewrite plus a before/after table across a covered task and a boundary task), and Complete + Kept-current ($14K + $2K/quarter, adds a quarterly re-measure against version drift). The $1,500 audit credits 100% into any tier. Payment is performance-gated: half to start, half only when the re-measured after-table beats the baseline the audit set. Details: https://damli40.github.io/thel0guy/build/

## Why teams bring me in (value props)

1. Your docs are now read by agents, not just developers — and I measure what they cost. A growing share of integrations start with someone pointing a coding agent at your docs; if that surface is ambiguous, the agent doesn't bounce — it ships the wrong config. In a controlled four-arm experiment on a smart-contract toolchain (same model, same prompt, pass/fail decided by rerunning the test suite in a clean container), the same task cost ~6x more per working integration without a curated docs layer, at 5x the wall clock. I write docs both humans and agents parse correctly, structure the agent-readable layer, and prove the difference with numbers. (This is the core differentiator.)
2. Your docs are your developer funnel. The hardest part of infra growth is getting people to understand the tech well enough to build on it. I write the explainer that turns "I don't get it" into "I shipped it."
3. I'm the rare overlap. Engineers who understand the protocol usually can't write; writers who can write usually don't understand it. I do both, with two years of public proof.
4. I translate infra into capital and adoption. My work has made fee-switch dynamics, DVN economics, and omnichain trust legible to thousands of sophisticated readers.

## Proof

- OFT Sentinel — an autonomous trust-intelligence agent and the entire narrative around it; WINNER of Mantle's Turing Test Hackathon ($17K across track first prize and the community vote, from 576 entries; announcement: https://x.com/Mantle_Official/status/2075596029408514552). The README and product are a writing sample: complex security mechanics, made clear. https://oft-sentinel.netlify.app
- The fee switch as a governance weapon — surfaced a competitive war most ZRO holders never saw. https://x.com/rookie_of_Ph/status/2013626693873783041
- DVNs are the ISPs of crypto — reframed a $100M infrastructure opportunity in one analogy. https://x.com/rookie_of_Ph/status/2001921578393833861
- Stats: 2 years as a top LayerZero voice; single posts regularly clearing thousands of views; winner of the Mantle Turing Test Hackathon, $17K, from 576 entries.

## Findings (measured, published in public)

- The agent cost experiment. The full 40-run writeup behind the audit: same model, same task, four context setups, pass/fail decided by rerunning the test suite in a clean container. Bad context cost about 6x per working integration, at 5x the clock. https://damli40.github.io/thel0guy/findings/agent-cost-experiment/
- Findings index (more measured findings publish here, including a forthcoming full public sample audit of a well-known toolchain): https://damli40.github.io/thel0guy/findings/
- Free check: a 10-question, 5-minute self-assessment of whether a doc surface is costing agent tokens, no sign-up. A low-commitment entry point that routes to the audit. https://damli40.github.io/thel0guy/check/

## What I deliver

Agent cost audit (measured, multi-arm, test-suite-verified); agent-readable doc structure (llms.txt / clean reads); integration & developer guides; protocol docs; technical explainers; strategic research content.
